_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=58 with 8 verified claims. MAJOR FACTUAL UPDATE: 2025 deficit actually came in at 3.7%, BELOW the 3.8% target — the issue's original 'slip past target' premise was wrong. Headline + hook + context + reframe rebuilt around the correct outturn. PFFRA Act 850 anchored — the 'no statutory framework' framing was INCORRECT; PFFRA provides statutory objectives and 3% medium-term target. View card recommendation 'establish a statutory framework' replaced with 'enforcement' framing (the statute exists; the open question is triggering the fiscal-adjustment mechanism).

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — Headline 'Risks Slipping Past the 3.8% Target' → '2025 Came In at 3.7%' (the actual outturn beat the projection).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — Hook 'forecast to slip past the 3.8% target' → '3.7% in 2025, beating the 3.8% projection'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'IMF and analyst houses note rising risks' (forecast tense) → 'Analyst forecasts had flagged risks ... Final outturn came in below those forecasts' (past tense, accurate).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Both have political costs' dropped from Fact 1 sub; replaced with neutral 'involve political trade-offs'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 2 'Even a one-notch downgrade raises borrowing costs across the sovereign curve' (assertive) → 'Downgrades are associated with higher yields and risk premia across the sovereign curve' (probabilistic).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 2 sub 'cascades through corporate bond markets and ultimately to mortgage rates and household credit costs' → 'The pass-through to corporate bond markets is well documented; specific mortgage and household-credit channels depend on local market conditions' (Stage 3: corporate-bond channel supported; mortgage pass-through not verified for Malaysia).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — Fact 3 'Medium-Term Fiscal Framework sets targets but has no statutory enforcement' replaced with 'Public Finance and Fiscal Responsibility Act 2023 (Act 850) provides statutory fiscal objectives and a 3-to-5-year MTFF' (PFFRA IS the statutory framework).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Parliament does not hold a structured post-budget hearing' replaced with PFFRA requirement that Finance Minister lay annual fiscal-outlook statement before Parliament.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — Reframe 'The story is not target missed' → 'The 2025 target was met. The open question is whether the glide path holds to the 3% PFFRA target without revenue or spending action.'
REPHRASED (Stage 3): Analogy 'dieter who keeps just over the goal weight' → 'household that hit its annual savings target' (matches the verified target-hit reality).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — View 'A statutory Fiscal Responsibility framework... is the structural fix' replaced with 'PFFRA already provides the statutory frame. The next test is enforcement: whether the fiscal-adjustment mechanism is triggered if the glide path slips.'

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 2024 deficit 4.1%; 4.3% original target; 3.8% 2025 target; analyst ~4.0% pre-outturn forecast; trajectory matters for credit rating; revenue-mobilisation vs spending-discipline trade-off.
